result.summary: Rotten Tomatoes exposes one known legacy HTTP API surface, the Rotten Tomatoes Public API at `http://api.rottentomatoes.com/api/public/v1.0`, documented by an unofficial OpenAPI spec and authenticated with an `apikey` query parameter; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
result.description: Rotten Tomatoes is a movie and TV discovery and reviews site that aggregates critic and audience scores, reviews, and related entertainment metadata. Its public website currently directs API and data-feed inquiries through a help desk / licensing contact path.

rt_api_key.label: Rotten Tomatoes API key
rt_api_key.setup: Use the [Rotten Tomatoes Help Desk](https://www.rottentomatoes.com/help_desk) and submit a request for integrating Rotten Tomatoes data via APIs or data feeds. The public OpenAPI spec for the legacy API requires an `apikey` query parameter, but the currently accessible public site only points developers to the help desk / licensing contact path rather than a self-serve key issuance page.
